name: MockAPI Studio - Postman Alternative
description: Transform mock-service into a comprehensive Postman alternative with OpenAPI import, Redocly-style API documentation, and enterprise-ready authentication

userStories.title: Database schema for Workspace and Project entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational database schema for workspaces and projects so that I can organize API collections hierarchically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Workspace table with: id, name, created_at, updated_at
Create Project table with: id, workspace_id, name, base_url, created_at, updated_at
Foreign key relationship between Project and Workspace
Default 'Personal' workspace seeded on first run

userStories.title: Database schema for Collection and Folder entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need collection and folder entities so that I can organize requests in a hierarchical structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Collection table with: id, project_id, name, description, auth_config (JSON), created_at
Create Folder table with: id, collection_id, parent_folder_id (self-referential), name, order
Support unlimited folder nesting via parent_folder_id

userStories.title: Database schema for SavedRequest entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a saved request entity to persist user-created API requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SavedRequest table with: id, folder_id, name, method, url, headers (JSON), body (JSON), auth (JSON), order, created_at, updated_at
Support all HTTP methods in method field

userStories.title: Database schema for Environment and EnvironmentVariable entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need environment entities to store variable configurations for different environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Environment table with: id, project_id, name, is_active, created_at
Create EnvironmentVariable table with: id, environment_id, key, value, is_secret
Only one environment can be active per project
Secret values should be stored (encryption to be added later)

userStories.title: Database schema for APIDefinition entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API definition entity to store imported OpenAPI specifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Create APIDefinition table with: id, project_id, name, spec_format (openapi3, postman), spec_content (TEXT), source_url, is_public, public_slug, created_at, updated_at

userStories.title: Database schema for RequestHistory entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a request history entity to log all sent requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create RequestHistory table with: id, workspace_id, method, url, request_data (JSON), response_data (JSON), status_code, response_time_ms, timestamp
Index on timestamp for efficient querying

userStories.title: Workspace CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage workspaces.

userStories.title: Project CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage projects within workspaces.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/projects/:id - Get single project with collections

userStories.title: Collection and Folder CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage collections and folders.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/projects/:id/collections - List collections with nested folders

userStories.title: SavedRequest CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage saved requests.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/requests/:id/move - Move request to different folder

userStories.title: Environment CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage environments and variables.
acceptanceCriteria: CRUD for environment variables within environment

userStories.title: HTTP Proxy endpoint for sending real requests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server-side proxy to send HTTP requests and avoid CORS issues.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/proxy/request - Proxy HTTP request to target URL
Support all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD)
Return response: status, headers, body, timing info

userStories.title: Request History API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to manage request history.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/history - List request history with pagination
History auto-logged when using proxy endpoint

userStories.title: OpenAPI 3.x import and parsing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to import OpenAPI specs so that I can auto-generate API definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Support both JSON and YAML input
Support import via: file upload, URL fetch, raw paste
Validate spec and return detailed errors
Store parsed spec in APIDefinition table

userStories.title: Postman Collection v2.1 import
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to import Postman collections so that I can migrate existing workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse Postman Collection v2.1 JSON format
Import requests with headers, body, auth
Map Postman folders to collection folders
Import Postman environments as environment presets

userStories.title: Generate mocks from API definition
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to generate mock endpoints from my API definition.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate example data from schema if no examples
Link generated mocks to source API definition

userStories.title: Variable substitution engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a variable substitution engine for {{variable}} syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Variable precedence: environment > collection > project > global
Return list of undefined variables as warnings
Utility function usable in proxy endpoint

userStories.title: Sidebar with Workspace/Project/Collection tree
userStories.description: As a user, I want a sidebar showing my workspace hierarchy so that I can navigate my API collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows workspace switcher dropdown at top
Tree view: Projects > Collections > Folders > Requests
Click request to open in request builder
Right-click context menu for CRUD actions
Visual indicators for method types (GET=green, POST=blue, etc.)
Verify in browser - sidebar renders correctly

userStories.title: Request Builder UI - URL and method
userStories.description: As a user, I want a request builder to construct HTTP requests.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP method dropdown (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD)
URL input field with {{variable}} syntax highlighting
Loading state while request is in flight
Verify in browser - can input URL and select method

userStories.title: Request Builder UI - Query params editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit query parameters in a key-value table.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser - params sync with URL

userStories.title: Request Builder UI - Headers editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit request headers in a key-value table.
acceptanceCriteria: Common header name autocomplete (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.)
Verify in browser - headers can be added/removed

userStories.title: Request Builder UI - Body editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit request body with multiple format options.
acceptanceCriteria: Format selector: none, JSON, form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded, raw, binary
JSON editor with syntax highlighting and validation
Form-data editor with file upload support
Raw text editor with content-type selector
Verify in browser - can switch formats and edit body

userStories.title: Request Builder UI - Auth editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure authentication for my requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth type selector: No Auth, API Key, Bearer Token, Basic Auth
API Key: key name, value, add to header or query
Basic Auth: username and password fields
Verify in browser - auth options render correctly

userStories.title: Response Viewer UI - Body display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view API responses with syntax highlighting.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON syntax highlighting with collapsible nodes
Verify in browser - JSON response renders with highlighting

userStories.title: Response Viewer UI - Metadata display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see response metadata like status, time, and size.
acceptanceCriteria: Status code badge with color (2xx green, 4xx orange, 5xx red)
Verify in browser - metadata displays correctly

userStories.title: Environment switcher UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly switch between environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser - can switch environments

userStories.title: Environment management UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage environments and their variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Secret toggle for sensitive values (masked display)
Verify in browser - can create and edit environments

userStories.title: Variable highlighting and autocomplete
userStories.description: As a user, I want {{variables}} to be highlighted and autocompleted.
acceptanceCriteria: {{variable}} syntax highlighted in URL, headers, body
Different color for defined vs undefined variables
Verify in browser - variables are highlighted

userStories.title: Save request dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save requests to my collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Save As option for existing requests
Verify in browser - can save request to collection

userStories.title: Request tabs UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to work with multiple requests in tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab shows: method badge, request name
Verify in browser - can open multiple request tabs

userStories.title: Request history panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view my request history.
acceptanceCriteria: List recent requests: method, URL, status, timestamp
Click to restore request to builder
Filter by date, method, status code
Verify in browser - history shows recent requests

userStories.title: OpenAPI import UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a UI to import OpenAPI specifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Import modal with tabs: File, URL, Paste
Verify in browser - can import OpenAPI file

userStories.title: Postman import UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a UI to import Postman collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Postman import tab in import modal
File upload for Postman collection JSON
Preview of what will be imported
Verify in browser - can import Postman collection

userStories.title: API Definition list and management UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and manage my imported API definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Show: name, format, endpoint count, last updated
Actions: View Docs, Generate Mocks, Re-import, Delete
Verify in browser - can view API definitions list

userStories.title: Redocly-style API documentation - Navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want beautiful API documentation with navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-panel layout: nav, content, code samples
Left nav: endpoint list grouped by tags
Verify in browser - navigation renders correctly

userStories.title: Redocly-style API documentation - Endpoint details
userStories.description: As a user, I want detailed endpoint documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Parameters table: path, query, header params
Response schemas for all status codes
Try it button to open in request builder
Verify in browser - endpoint details render

userStories.title: Redocly-style API documentation - Code samples
userStories.description: As a user, I want code samples in multiple languages.
acceptanceCriteria: Language tabs: cURL, JavaScript, Python, Go, Ruby
Verify in browser - code samples render correctly

userStories.title: Redocly-style API documentation - Schema viewer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse data model schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: List all schemas from OpenAPI components
Property table: name, type, required, description
Verify in browser - schemas render correctly

userStories.title: Public documentation sharing
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want to share API docs via public URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate shareable URL for public docs
Custom slug option for clean URLs
Public docs page without app chrome
Verify in browser - public docs URL works

userStories.title: Mock generation UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to generate mocks from my API definition via UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate Mocks button on API definition
Verify in browser - can generate mocks

userStories.title: Response comparison UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to compare responses between requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser - diff view works

userStories.title: OAuth 2.0 - Authorization Code flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to authenticate with OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Configuration: auth URL, token URL, client ID, client secret, scopes

userStories.title: OAuth 2.0 - Client Credentials flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to authenticate with OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Configuration: token URL, client ID, client secret, scopes
Get Token button (no user interaction needed)

userStories.title: Keycloak SSO integration
userStories.description: As an enterprise user, I want to login with Keycloak SSO.
acceptanceCriteria: Keycloak configuration page (realm, client ID, URLs)

userStories.title: Drag-and-drop reordering in sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reorganize my collections with drag-and-drop.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag folders to reorder or nest
Prevent invalid drops (e.g., folder into itself)
Verify in browser - drag and drop works

userStories.title: Keyboard shortcuts
userStories.description: As a power user, I want keyboard shortcuts for common actions.

userStories.title: Migrate existing mock data to new schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to migrate existing mock-service data to the new schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration script to move existing services to projects
Map existing endpoints to saved requests

userStories.title: Cloud sync infrastructure (optional)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the infrastructure for optional cloud sync.
